_doc: Provenance manifest for Carlisle source-document derived exam content (PDS and install/field guides). This file is NOT loaded by /api/exam at runtime. It exists for traceability, QA review, and future-author guidance.
_internalSecurityNote: INTERNAL ONLY. The Carlisle PDF files referenced by this manifest live under sources/manufacturers/carlisle/ (in pds/ and install-guides/) and MUST NEVER be exposed publicly via /api/book, /api/books, the Library page, or any other RoofVault endpoint. They are manufacturer-published collateral © Carlisle and are used here solely as source material to derive original RoofVault exam questions. The locked-library helper at api/_helpers/locked-library-blobs.js already contains a 'MfgCarlisle' pattern that would 403 any blob whose name matches /\bcarlisle\b.*\b(?:manual|guide|specification|spec)\b/ at the /api/book endpoint, and these PDFs are stored only in sources/ (not in the Azure blob container backing /api/book or /api/books), giving defense-in-depth. Do not upload these PDFs to Azure storage. Do not link to them from the frontend. Do not import them from any runtime module under /api or the root frontend bundle.
copyrightNotice: Carlisle Product Data Sheets and field/install guides are © Carlisle. Used internally for RoofVault exam-content derivation. Questions and explanations authored from this material must paraphrase technical content; do not copy marketing prose. Standard test references (ASTM, UL, FM, NSF, CRRC, LEED, Title 24) and numeric specifications cited from these documents are factual data of the kind specifiers routinely cite in their own work.
useRestrictions: Do NOT serve the PDF files through /api/book, /api/books, or any public route.
Do NOT upload the PDF files to the Azure blob container that backs /api/book or /api/books.
Do NOT copy verbatim marketing prose (headers, taglines, brand-promise paragraphs) into question text or explanations.
Do NOT include trademark symbols (TM, R) in question text; trademarks are acknowledged here in the trademarks array instead.
Long verbatim excerpts (more than a short phrase) belong only in this manifest's optional 'quote' field for internal QA review, never in question text or explanations.

Use 'cite' on each generated question in the form: Carlisle <short product> <doc-type> - p. <page>, <section heading>. Examples: 'Carlisle Sure-Weld TPO PDS - p. 2, Precautions'; 'Carlisle Thermoplastics Field Guide - p. 36, Section 4 - Common Installation Issues'.

_sourceEntryShape.sourceKey: stable short id used by question entries to reference a source PDF
_sourceEntryShape.normalizedFileName: kebab-case filename as stored in this repo under pds/ or install-guides/
_sourceEntryShape.originalFileName: the filename as it arrived on Desktop, preserved here for traceability
_sourceEntryShape.footerDate: YYYY-MM-DD parsed from the document footer revision date
_sourceEntryShape.copyrightYear: year shown in the document footer
_sourceEntryShape.reprintCode: Carlisle reprint/SKU code from the document footer (e.g. 605436 / EPDM-1117)
_sourceEntryShape.productShortName: human display string for the 'cite' field on questions
_sourceEntryShape.productFullName: the product or document name as it appears in the source heading
_sourceEntryShape.thicknessesMil: list of available membrane thicknesses (or union across systems for multi-product sources)
_sourceEntryShape.intendedBankKey: the planned /api/exam bank key that will be authored from this source (singular form; use when a source feeds exactly one bank)
_sourceEntryShape.intendedBankKeys: list of planned /api/exam bank keys (plural form; use when a single source feeds multiple banks)
_sourceEntryShape.fileSizeBytes: byte size of the PDF as stored
_sourceEntryShape.sha256: SHA-256 hex of the PDF as stored
_sourceEntryShape.sectionHeadings: list of section headings present in the source, used as the 'section' anchor in question citations

sure-seal-epdm-kleen-non-reinforced.originalFileName: Sure-Seal EPDM Kleen Non-Reinforced Membranes Product Data Sheet.pdf
sure-seal-epdm-kleen-non-reinforced.notes: Type I non-reinforced EPDM per ASTM D4637. 90-mil supports 30-year warranty assemblies. Primary install design referenced: Design A (Fully Adhered). Hand-applied alternative seam: SecurTAPE.

sure-tough-epdm-reinforced.notes: Type II reinforced EPDM per ASTM D4637. Internal polyester scrim. Supports Design MFS (Mechanically Fastened), Design MR (Metal Retrofit), and Design A (Fully Adhered). 60 percent greater puncture resistance vs non-reinforced (ASTM D5635 / Federal Method 2031). Desktop original filename had stray spaces around the hyphen.

sure-weld-tpo-reinforced.originalFileName: TPO-2012 Sure-Weld TPO Reinforced Membrane PDS_01-28-26.pdf
sectionHeadings: Extreme Testing for Severe Climates (ASTM D6878)
Radiative Properties for Cool Roof Rating Council (CRRC) and LEED
sure-weld-tpo-reinforced.notes: Heat-weldable TPO with OctaGuard XT weathering package. Supports Fully Adhered, Mechanically Fastened, and Induction-Welded designs. Sure-Weld Extra is the 80-mil variant. APEEL Protective Film is an optional factory-applied film with specific handling rules. Maximum sustained membrane temperature 160 F. Some PDF tools report 0 pages (PDF 1.7 with non-standard page table) but content is fully extractable; downstream tooling should rely on the section headings list above rather than page indices alone.

carlisle-thermoplastics-field-guide.productFullName: Carlisle Thermoplastics Field Guide (Sure-Weld TPO and Sure-Flex PVC install reference)
sectionHeadings: Execution / Installation Procedures - Sure-Weld TPO General
Execution / Installation Procedures - Sure-Weld TPO Execution
Execution / Installation Procedures - Sure-Flex PVC General
Execution / Installation Procedures - Sure-Flex PVC Execution
Vapor / Air Barrier Details (V-8 and V-12 families)
carlisle-thermoplastics-field-guide.notes: 246-page Carlisle install/QA reference covering Sure-Weld TPO and Sure-Flex PVC (plus Spectro-Weld, KEE HP, Induction-Welded Grid, Metal Retrofit, SAT, Adhered, and FleeceBACK variants). This single source feeds two planned banks: carlisle_sure_weld_tpo_field_install (TPO install judgment) and carlisle_sure_flex_pvc_field_install (PVC install judgment). When authoring questions, do NOT repeat concepts already covered by the carlisle_sure_weld_tpo_reinforced PDS bank (notably CARSW-002/003 APEEL razor + 90-day window, CARSW-006 max sustained 160 F, CARSW-007 oxidation per 18 F). The Field Guide's install procedures, daily seam-probing, common installation issues, staging/storage, and PVC-specific welding parameters are net-new content. Reprint code 611463 / CST-9994. Footer marker: 'Thermoplastics Field Guide - 09.06.23'. Section anchors marked 'JULY 2023' on internal section headers.

_bankEntryShape.bankFile: repo-relative path to the question-bank module
_bankEntryShape.displayTitle: the dropdown label exactly as it will appear
_bankEntryShape.idPrefix: stable per-bank ID prefix used on every question in that bank
_bankEntryShape.source: sourceKey of the PDS this bank is derived from
_bankEntryShape.targetQuestionCount: honest premium count agreed with product owner; no filler beyond this

carlisle_sure_weld_tpo_field_install.notes: Wired Step 5. Install-judgment angle complementary to carlisle_sure_weld_tpo_reinforced (PDS bank). Does NOT repeat APEEL razor/90-day, 160 F sustained-temp, or oxidation-per-18 F concepts already in the PDS bank.

carlisle_sure_flex_pvc_field_install.notes: Wired Step 5. Covers Sure-Flex PVC and Sure-Flex KEE HP install judgment (PVC Membrane Cleaner 15-min flash-off, 1,094 F auto-welder starting parameters, PVC bleed-out interpretation flip vs TPO, aged-membrane bottom-side weld fallback, PVC-specific probe-tip geometry, Section 7 test-weld delamination indicator, scorched-membrane diagnostic, PVC Metal Retrofit Half Sheets/Linear Induction Welded options).

_questionEntryShape.page: page number in the PDS where the claim appears
_questionEntryShape.section: section heading in the PDS where the claim appears
_questionEntryShape.quote: OPTIONAL short verbatim excerpt for internal QA only; never copied into question text
_questionEntryShape.tags: list of free-form topic tags (e.g. cold-weather-splice, scrim-reinforcement, heat-welding)

CARSS-001.section: Installation (Design A) / ASTM D4637 Type I
CARSS-001.quote: Sure-Seal Kleen 45-mil, 60-mil, and 90-mil membranes are utilized primarily in Design A (Fully Adhered roofing systems). Sure-Seal Kleen Non-Reinforced EPDM Membrane meets or exceeds the minimum requirements set forth by ASTM D4637 for Type I non-reinforced EPDM single-ply roofing membranes.

CARSS-002.quote: Follow these steps for splicing in temperatures below 40 F (5 C): 1. Heat the primed area of the bottom membrane with a hot-air gun as the top sheet with Factory-Applied Tape is applied and pressed into place. 2. Prior to rolling the splice area with a 2-inch-wide steel hand roller, apply heat to the top side of the membrane with a hot-air gun. The heated surface should be hot to the touch. Be careful not to burn or blister the membrane.

CARSS-003.quote: Membranes with Factory-Applied Tape should not be exposed to prolonged jobsite storage temperatures in excess of 90 F (32 C), otherwise the shelf life of the tape may be affected. When membranes with Factory-Applied Tape are used in warm, sunny weather, shade the tape end of the rolls until ready to use. Factory-Applied Tape has a shelf life of one year.

CARSS-004.quote: Carlisle's 30-year Sure-Seal EPDM roof system features a thicker, more durable membrane complemented by enhanced details and accessories. Carlisle's 90-mil EPDM is used for 30-year warranty installations to provide long-term value and performance. In addition to 30 years of guaranteed protection, this system is available with warranties to cover hail, accidental punctures, and wind speeds up to 120 mph.

CARSS-005.quote: Numerous studies and real-world experience confirm that Sure-Seal EPDM's 465% elongation and weathering resistance result in superior hail damage resistance (UL 2218 Class 4 rating). Elongation, Ultimate, min, %: 300 spec / 465 typical (ASTM D412).

CARSS-006.quote: Design A (Fully Adhered Roofing System): insulation is mechanically fastened or adhered to the roof deck. The substrate and membrane are coated with the appropriate Carlisle bonding adhesive. The membrane is then rolled into place and broomed down.

CARST-001.quote: 60% greater resistance to punctures (as measured by ASTM D5635 and Federal Method 2031) compared to non-reinforced membranes. Internally reinforced sheets provide excellent resistance to punctures, tears, and scuffs that can be caused by maintenance traffic, backed by the industry's longest puncture warranty.

CARST-002.quote: Design MFS (Mechanically Fastened) and Design MR (Metal Retrofit): insulation is mechanically fastened to the roof deck and membrane is secured with seam fastening plates or bars and fasteners. ... Sheet flutter/noise may occur on mechanically fastened systems.

CARST-003.section: Typical Properties and Characteristics (Thickness Over Scrim)
CARST-003.quote: Thickness Over Scrim, min, in. (mm) - .045 / .060 Annex 0.015 (0.381) / 0.016 typical / 0.020 typical. .075 typical 0.032 (0.81).

CARST-004.quote: Sure-Tough reinforced EPDM membrane meets or exceeds the minimum requirements set forth by ASTM D4637 for Type II reinforced EPDM single-ply roofing membranes.

CARST-005.quote: Breaking Strength, min, lbf (N) - ASTM D751, Grab Method, .045/.060: 90 (400) min / 210 (930) typical.

CARST-006.quote: Carlisle offers 5' (1.5 m) and 6.5' (1.98 m) wide sheets, ideal for use as perimeter sheets and to achieve certain uplift ratings, in 45- and 60-mil thicknesses.

CARST-007.section: Overview (UL Class A up to 3-inch slope)
CARST-007.quote: Sure-Tough membranes are formulated with fire retardants to inhibit the spread of flame and meet or exceed UL Class A requirements for slopes up to 3 inches per foot (76.2 mm), depending on the assembly.

CARSW-001.quote: Fully-Adhered - membrane is adhered to a suitable substrate utilizing an appropriate bonding adhesive. Mechanically Fastened - membrane is attached to the roof deck over a suitable substrate utilizing plates and fasteners which are overlapped with membrane. Induction-Welded - membrane is attached over a suitable substrate via an induction welding tool being placed over the membrane where a fastened TPO induction welding plate is located to weld the two components together.

CARSW-002.quote: Do not use razor blades or other sharp tools to cut the APEEL Protective Film while it is still adhered to the TPO membrane as damage to the underlying membrane may occur. Pull the protective film away from the membrane prior to cutting. A static electric charge may develop when removing APEEL Protective Film from the surface of the membrane sheet. To avoid the possibility of ignition, lids must be closed on any flammable products and a fire extinguisher should be readily available.

CARSW-003.quote: In areas that do not require heat-welding, the APEEL Protective Film can be left in place for up to 90 days without degrading due to its excellent heat- and UV-resistance.

CARSW-004.quote: Up to 60% fewer seams vs. 10-foot TPO. Fewer rolls to load and stage. Fewer rolls to position, kick-out, and align. Fewer seams to weld, probe, and inspect, saving considerable time during installation. Fewer T-joint patches to install on each roof. Less waste and trash from packaging.

CARSW-005.quote: Sure-Weld High Slope (HS) membrane is formulated with additional flame retardant for higher-slope fire code approvals. HS formula opens assemblies for slopes greater than 1/2 inch over combustible and non-combustible deck types where standard TPO is restricted.

CARSW-006.quote: Maximum sustained temperature not to exceed 160 F (71 C) for TPO membrane.

CARSW-007.quote: Heat Aging accelerates the oxidation rate that roughly doubles for each 18 F (10 C) increase in roof membrane temperature. Oxidation (reaction with oxygen) is one of the primary chemical degradation mechanisms of roofing materials.

CARSW-008.quote: Sure-Weld TPO was tested for dynamic puncture resistance per ASTM D5635-04 using the most recently modified impact head. 45-mil (1.14 mm) was watertight after an impact energy of 12.5 J (9.2 ft-lbf) and 60-mil (1.52 mm) was watertight after 22.5 J (16.6 ft-lbf). 80-mil (2.03 mm) Extra was watertight after an impact energy of 30.0 J (22.1 ft-lbf).

CARSW-009.quote: Membrane that has been exposed to the weather must be prepared with Weathered Membrane Cleaner prior to hot-air welding.

CARSW-010.section: Precautions (color fading) + Sustainable Attributes (Title 24, NSF P151)
CARSW-010.quote: Color membranes will 'fade' over time mainly due to the ultraviolet portion of sunlight. Since most roof surfaces are exposed to variable sunlight, some areas will be more susceptible to color changes caused by UV fading. Warranties for color membranes do not cover fading of colors. California Title 24 compliant (White and Tan only). NSF P151 certification for rainwater catchment (White only, produced in Tooele, UT and Carlisle, PA).

CARTW-001.section: Section 4 - Common Installation Issues (Reduce the chance for cold welds on TPO membranes)
CARTW-001.quote: A good starting point for welding all types and thickness of Sure-Weld TPO on auto-welders is 1,000 F at 12'/minute with airflow at 100%. ... A proper hot air welded seam has no voids or wrinkles and must be at least 1.5 inches wide.

CARTW-002.quote: Hand welding flashing the welder should be set at #7 and for membrane set at #8. A proper hot air welded seam has no voids or wrinkles and must be at least 1.5 inches wide.

CARTW-003.quote: Perform a test weld at the start of each morning and afternoon on a piece of like membrane over the same substrate.

CARTW-004.section: Section 4 - Common Installation Issues (24-hour seam-cleaning rule)
CARTW-004.quote: Seams that are not welded within 24 hours should be cleaned with a Splice Wipe soaked with Weathered Membrane Cleaner regardless of conditions. Allow the cleaned area to vent for at least 10 minutes prior to welding again.

CARTW-005.section: Section 4 - Common Installation Issues (aged membrane welding)
CARTW-005.quote: Welding aged membrane (over 1-year and longer) may require the use of a Primer Pad and Weathered Membrane Cleaner. Work up a slurry using the Primer Pad and Weather[ed] Membrane Cleaner. Using a Splice Wipe remove the residue from the cleaned area. Wipe the membrane again using a clean Splice Wipe and Weathered Membrane Cleaner. Allow the cleaned area to vent for at least 10 minutes prior to welding.

CARTW-006.quote: Probing seams must be done once hot air welds have thoroughly cooled (min. 20 minutes). Hot air weld seams must be probed throughout the day to check seam quality and to make proper adjustments to hot air welding equipment. The repair of deficiencies must be done routinely throughout the day but no later than the end of each workday.

CARTW-007.quote: Membrane securement must be installed at perimeters of each roof level, curbs, skylights, expansion joints and all inside deck angle changes greater than 2 inches in 12.

CARTW-008.section: Section 4 - Common Installation Issues (Membrane Securement - penetrations)
CARTW-008.quote: Membrane securement is also required around all pipe penetrations and sealant pockets regardless of size on mechanically fastened systems. On adhered systems only when the pipe diameter exceeds 18 inches in size or the sealant pocket exceeds 12 inches in size is membrane securement required.

CARTW-009.quote: Overheating of welds is evident when bleed-out occurs. Bleed-out is the dark underside of the membrane that begins to melt and flow. Bleed-out will not occur with Sure-Weld TPO membrane if properly welded. If overheating is evident, a piece of non-reinforced flashing may be used for a repair. The non-reinforced flashing should overlay the deficiency 2 inches in all directions and be welded 100%.

CARTW-010.section: Section 5 - Staging and Storage Best Practices (Pressure-Sensitive Products)
CARTW-010.quote: TPO pressure sensitive products to include TPO P.S. Coverstrip, Warning Strip and RUSS must not be prolonged to jobsite storage temperatures more than 90 F may affect product shelf life. ... Storage and use of TPO PS Coverstrip at temperatures below 40 F will result in a loss of adhesive tack, and in extreme cases will result in an inadequate bond to the substrate. Overnight storage must be available to keep the temperature of the TPO PS Coverstrip at a minimum of 40 F. Hot boxes for jobsite storage must be provided to maintain a minimum product temperature of 40 F. TPO PS Coverstrip must be stored in a dry area.

CARTW-011.section: Section 5 - Staging and Storage Best Practices (Membrane and Flashing - SAT TPO)
CARTW-011.quote: SAT TPO membrane must be installed at ambient temperatures above 50 F (10 C).

CARTW-012.section: Section 1 - Description of Systems (Sure-Weld TPO Metal Retrofit Mechanically Fastened)
CARTW-012.quote: The Sure-Weld TPO Metal Retrofit Mechanically Fastened Roofing System: White, Gray and Tan Membrane Colors Available; 4', 6', 8', 10', and 12' Widths Available; 60-mil and 80-mil Thicknesses Available; Option 1 - PS RUSS Strip; Option 2 - Linear Induction Welded.

CARPV-001.section: Section 4 - Common Installation Issues (Reduce the chance for cold welds on PVC membranes)
CARPV-001.quote: When cleaning PVC it is important to allow the solvents from the PVC Membrane Cleaner to thoroughly flash-off prior to welding. This will take up to 15 minutes to occur. If this procedure is rushed the PVC membrane may exhibit cold welding. Cold welding is defined as a weld that is not properly fused together, allowing the weld area to separate after natural expansion and contraction occurs in the roofing system.

CARPV-002.section: Section 4 - Common Installation Issues (PVC auto-welder starting parameters)
CARPV-002.quote: A good starting point for welding all types and thickness of Sure-Flex PVC on auto-welders is 1,094 F at 8.9'/minute. Using the Leister V-2 welder the speed can be utilized up to 10.2'/minute.

CARPV-003.section: Section 4 - Common Installation Issues (PVC bleed-out interpretation)
CARPV-003.quote: A proper weld for PVC will exhibit a little bit of bleed-out at the overlap step-off. Bleed-out is when the darker bottom ply actually starts to flow from the heat melting the sheet.

CARPV-004.section: Section 4 - Common Installation Issues (aged PVC welding)
CARPV-004.quote: Welding PVC membranes that are 5-7 years or older category may be difficult to achieve proper fusion. You may have to utilize the bottom side of the older sheet that has not seen weathering and is generally more stable for welding.

CARPV-005.section: Section 4 - Common Installation Issues (PVC seam probing)
CARPV-005.quote: When probing PVC make sure the membrane is thoroughly cooled. Proceed with probing making sure the probe point is duller and has a flatter point compared to TPO probing.

CARPV-006.section: Section 1 - Description of Systems (Sure-Flex PVC and Sure-Flex KEE HP product families)
CARPV-006.quote: The Sure-Flex PVC Mechanically Fastened Roofing System: White, Gray, Tan, Light Gray, and Slate Gray Colors Available; 40.5\", 5', 81\", and 10' Widths Available; 50-mil, 60-mil, and 80-mil Thicknesses Available. The Sure-Flex KEE HP Mechanically Fastened Roofing System: White, Gray, Tan, and Light Gray Colors Available; 5' and 10' Widths Available; 50-mil, 60-mil, and 80-mil Thicknesses Available.

CARPV-007.quote: For Sure-Weld membrane, Weathered Membrane Cleaner can be used to clean the surface of the membrane. For Sure-Flex Membrane, PVC Membrane Cleaner can be used to clean the surface of the membrane.

CARPV-008.quote: Peel the test sample apart after it has thoroughly cooled (approximately 10 minutes) and examine for a consistent 1.5 inch wide minimum weld. Delamination of the membrane from the scrim-reinforcement is an indication of a properly welded seam.

CARPV-009.section: Section 7 - Daily Procedures (Test Welds, seam-quality troubleshooting)
CARPV-009.quote: Discolored or scorched membrane - Increase speed or decrease temperature setting if membrane discolors.

CARPV-010.section: Section 1 - Description of Systems (Sure-Flex PVC and KEE HP Metal Retrofit Mechanically Fastened)
CARPV-010.quote: The Sure-Flex PVC and KEE HP Metal Retrofit Mechanically Fastened Roofing System: ... Option 1 - Half Sheets; Option 2 - Linear Induction Welded.
